[B07.000] - Environment - Description of circular economy principles ⮌

YES
Undertaking applies circular economy principles
The undertaking applies circular economy principles by delivering its services digitally and minimising the use of materials. IT equipment is acquired for long-term use and replaced only when technically necessary. The undertaking does not engage in physical production or material-intensive processes.
Description of how circular economy principles are applied

[C01.000] - General information - Strategy: Business Model and Sustainability – Related Initiatives ⮌

The undertaking provides sustainability reporting advisory services, including VSME reporting solutions, as well as the development of data-driven management practices and Power BI dashboards for SMEs.
Description of significant groups of products and/or services offered
The primary market consists of Finnish small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), particularly within professional and service-oriented sectors. The undertaking operates on a business-to-business (B2B) basis.
Description of significant market(s) the undertaking operates in e.g. B2B, wholesale, retail, countries
The undertaking’s clients are small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Operations are based on direct client relationships without intermediaries. Key suppliers relate to standard digital tools and software services used in the provision of advisory services.
Description of main business relationships (e.g. key suppliers, customers distribution channels) e.g. key suppliers, customers distribution channels and consumers
Sustainability considerations are integrated into the undertaking’s business model. The services support clients in their transition towards more sustainable operations. Activities are primarily digital and resource-light, and sustainability-related information is used in the ongoing development of services.
Description of key elements of strategy that relates to or affects sustainability issues

[C02.000] - General information - Description of practices, policies and/or future initiatives for transitioning towards a more sustainable economy ⮌

Sustainability is integrated into Fokuna’s business model. Operations are based on a digital and resource-light approach. During the reporting period, monitoring of energy consumption was initiated and preparations were made to establish a systematic recording of business travel. In equipment procurement, used or long-life solutions are preferred where feasible. Responsibility for sustainability-related practices and their development lies with the entrepreneur.
Description of practices, policies and/or future initiatives
Miska-Matias Kettunen
Most senior level within its employees that is accountable for policies
No separate quantitative targets were established during the reporting period. The undertaking’s objective is to progressively develop a more systematic approach to monitoring sustainability-related information as operations grow, taking into account the principle of proportionality.
Description of the target of practices, policies or future initiatives

[D99.000] - Other and/or entity specific information ⮌

The reporting period covers 6 October 2025 to 31 December 2025. The undertaking commenced its operations during this period. No turnover was generated during the reporting period. Consequently, greenhouse gas emission intensity per turnover is not considered a meaningful performance indicator for this reporting period. Financial figures are based on the accounting information available at the reporting date. The final year-end financial statements had not yet been prepared at the time of reporting.
Disclosure of any other general and/or entity specific information on the reporting period
During the reporting period, total electricity consumption amounted to 0.04951 MWh (49.51 kWh). Scope 2 greenhouse gas emissions (location-based) were calculated using the average emission factor for electricity generation in Finland (26 gCO2/kWh). Based on this methodology, Scope 2 emissions amount to 0.001287 tCO2e. Values in the report are presented with two decimal precision; therefore, Scope 2 emissions are displayed as 0.00 tCO2e, although the unrounded value is included in the total emissions calculation.

Entity-specific Scope 3 information is disclosed only for selected categories considered relevant and for which sufficiently reliable data was available during the reporting period. On this basis, the undertaking identified Category 2 Capital Goods as the most relevant disclosed Scope 3 category. This category includes the Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 14 laptop and the Logitech MX Master 4 mouse acquired for use in the business during the reporting period. The estimate for the laptop is based on the manufacturer’s published life cycle assessment document LCA Results for Notebook, according to which the total life cycle climate impact of the Samsung Galaxy Book5 Pro 14 is 240.0 kg CO2e over a four-year use period. The estimate for the mouse is based on the manufacturer’s published carbon footprint data for Logitech MX Master 3S Bluetooth Edition, which reports a product carbon footprint of 7.78 kg CO2e and was used as a proxy for the Logitech MX Master 4 mouse, as product-specific carbon footprint data for the exact model was not available at the reporting date. On this basis, total disclosed Scope 3 emissions under Category 2 Capital Goods amount to 0.248 tCO2e. The estimate includes some uncertainty, particularly regarding the use of proxy data for the mouse and possible regional or product variant differences, but the values used were considered the most reliable available sources for the purpose of this report.
Disclosure of any other environmental and/or entity specific environmental disclosures
The undertaking had no employees during the reporting period and therefore no additional social disclosures are applicable.
Disclosure of any other social and/or entity specific social disclosures
The undertaking is a sole proprietorship and does not have a separate governance body. Governance responsibilities rest with the entrepreneur.
Disclosure of any other governance and/or entity specific governance disclosures
